summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is a polyatomic ion?

A charged group of two or more covalently bonded atoms is correct. This is a key concept in Polyatomic Ions.

What is the formula for the sulfate ion?

SO₄²⁻. This is an important concept in Polyatomic Ions.

What is the name for NaNO₃?

Sodium nitrate. This is an important concept in Polyatomic Ions.

What is the formula for the hydroxide ion?

OH⁻. This is an important concept in Polyatomic Ions.

What is the charge of the ammonium ion?

+1. This is an important concept in Polyatomic Ions.

What is the formula for calcium carbonate?

CaCO₃. This is an important concept in Polyatomic Ions.

What is the name for K₂SO₄?

Potassium sulfate. This is an important concept in Polyatomic Ions.

What is the name for NH₄Cl?

Ammonium chloride. This is an important concept in Polyatomic Ions.

What is the formula for the phosphate ion?

PO₄³⁻. This is an important concept in Polyatomic Ions.

What is the name for Mg(OH)₂?

Magnesium hydroxide. This is an important concept in Polyatomic Ions.

What is the formula for aluminum sulfate?

Al₂(SO₄)₃. This is an important concept in Polyatomic Ions.

What is the name for Fe(NO₃)₃?

Iron(III) nitrate. This is an important concept in Polyatomic Ions.

What is the charge of the permanganate ion (MnO₄)?

-1. This is an important concept in Polyatomic Ions.

What is the name for Li₂CO₃?

Lithium carbonate. This is an important concept in Polyatomic Ions.

What is the formula for barium phosphate?

Ba₃(PO₄)₂. This is an important concept in Polyatomic Ions.

What is the formula for the acetate ion?

Acetate = CH₃COO⁻ (ethanoate in IUPAC). Charge = −1. Found in vinegar (acetic acid = CH₃COOH).

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: The acetate ion (CH₃COO⁻) is an example of:

A polyatomic ion containing carbon. This is an important concept in Polyatomic Ions.
